Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Data Center revenue reached a quarterly record driven by strong demand for Hopper architecture graphics processing units from hyperscale cloud providers and enterprise customers building artificial intelligence infrastructure.

Gaming revenue declined year-over-year as the channel worked through elevated inventory from the prior year, though sequential improvement in sell-through indicated normalization was progressing as anticipated.

Gross margin expanded substantially relative to the prior year, reflecting product mix shift toward higher-value data center configurations and improved manufacturing yields on our leading-node process.

We expect supply constraints to ease progressively over the coming quarters as our manufacturing partners ramp capacity and our supply chain partners qualify new substrates and components.

Research and development expenses increased significantly as we accelerated investment in next-generation GPU architectures, networking technology, and software platforms for the artificial intelligence ecosystem.

Item 3. Other Information
